summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

pH = −log[H⁺]. If [H⁺] = 0.001, what is the pH?

pH = −log(0.001) = −log(10⁻³) = −(−3) = 3.

Decibels: dB = 10 log(I/I₀). If I = 100I₀, what is the decibel level?

dB = 10 log(100) = 10 × 2 = 20 dB.

Using the Rule of 72, what is the approximate doubling time for an investment growing at 8% per year?

The Rule of 72 gives doubling time ≈ 72 ÷ 8 = 9 years.

Using the continuous doubling time formula T = ln(2)/r, what is the doubling time at 10% continuous growth?

T = ln(2)/0.10 ≈ 0.693/0.10 = 6.93 years.

$2,000 is invested at 5% compounded annually for 2 years. What is the final amount?

A = 2,000(1.05)² = 2,000 × 1.1025 = $2,205.

What is the pH scale?

The pH scale uses the formula pH = −log[H⁺], compressing a wide range of hydrogen-ion concentrations into values typically between 0 and 14.

What is the Richter scale?

The Richter scale assigns a magnitude using R = log(I/I₀), so each unit increase represents a 10-fold increase in wave amplitude.

What is a decibel?

Decibels (dB) use the formula dB = 10 log(I/I₀) to express sound intensity on a logarithmic scale, making very large ranges of intensities manageable.

What is compound interest?

Compound interest earns interest on interest, modeled by A = P(1 + r/n)^(nt), making it grow faster than simple interest.

What is continuous compounding?

Continuous compounding is the mathematical limit of compounding ever more frequently, resulting in the formula A = Pe^(rt) using Euler's number e.

What is the Rule of 72?

The Rule of 72 is a quick estimate: divide 72 by the annual percentage growth rate to approximate how many years it takes for an investment to double.

What is logistic growth?

Logistic growth models populations with limited resources: fast initial growth that decelerates as it nears the carrying capacity L.

What is the carrying capacity in a logistic model P(t) = L/(1 + be^(−kt))?

L is the carrying capacity, the upper limit that the population approaches as t increases. The denominator ensures P(t) never exceeds L.

What is exponential regression?

Exponential regression finds the best-fit exponential curve y = abˣ for data where each successive ratio of y-values is approximately constant.

The pH of lemon juice is approximately 2. What is the hydrogen-ion concentration [H⁺]?

pH = 2 means −log[H⁺] = 2, so [H⁺] = 10⁻² = 0.01.

A population follows logistic growth: P(t) = 10,000/(1 + 49e^(−0.3t)). What is the carrying capacity?

In the logistic model P(t) = L/(1 + be^(−kt)), the carrying capacity is L = 10,000, the value P(t) approaches as t → ∞.

$10,000 is invested at 6% compounded quarterly for 5 years. What is the approximate final amount? (Use (1.015)²⁰ ≈ 1.3469.)

A = 10,000(1 + 0.06/4)^(4×5) = 10,000(1.015)²⁰ ≈ 10,000 × 1.3469 = $13,469.

A biologist studies a population modeled by P(t) = 5,000/(1 + 24e^(−0.5t)). What is the initial population P(0)?

P(0) = 5,000/(1 + 24e⁰) = 5,000/(1 + 24) = 5,000/25 = 200.

A student uses exponential regression and finds the model y = 3(2)ˣ. What is the value of y when x = 5?

y = 3(2)⁵ = 3 × 32 = 96.
